task_completion.description: Create the R script file at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open text editor or IDE (like RStudio or VS Code) editing a file named 'credit_risk_analysis.R' located in '/home/ga/RProjects/'.

task_completion.description: Load data, perform a 70/30 split with set.seed(123), and train a Random Forest model on the 'default' target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for R code containing 'read.csv' for 'german_credit.csv', 'set.seed(123)', logic for a 70/30 train-test split (e.g., using 'sample' or 'createDataPartition'), and a Random Forest training function (e.g., 'randomForest()' or 'ranger()') with 'default' as the target variable.

task_completion.description: Evaluate model performance (Accuracy and AUC) and save to model_metrics.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for R code calculating Accuracy and AUC (e.g., using 'predict', 'confusionMatrix', 'roc', or 'auc') and a command like 'write.csv' saving these metrics to '/home/ga/RProjects/output/model_metrics.csv'. Alternatively, look for the generated CSV file in a file explorer or terminal.

task_completion.description: Generate and save a variable importance plot for the top 10 predictors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for R code using functions like 'vip()', 'varImpPlot()', or 'importance()' specifically limited to the top 10 predictors, and saving the output to '/home/ga/RProjects/output/variable_importance.png' (using 'png()', 'ggsave()', etc.). Alternatively, look for the actual image file being viewed.

task_completion.description: Generate and save a partial dependence plot for 'months_loan_duration'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for R code using functions like 'partial()', 'pdp::partial()', or 'model_profile()' targeting the feature 'months_loan_duration', and saving the output to '/home/ga/RProjects/output/pdp_duration.png'. Alternatively, look for the actual image file being viewed.

integrity.description: The agent actually executed the R script to generate the outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for signs of execution in the R console or terminal (e.g., output logs, progress bars, or command prompts showing 'Rscript credit_risk_analysis.R'), rather than just writing text into a file without running it.

integrity.description: The agent calculated the metrics dynamically rather than hardcoding fake values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the script computes Accuracy and AUC from the model's predictions on the test set. There should be no hardcoded numeric values (e.g., 'Accuracy <- 0.75') written directly to the metrics CSV.

integrity.description: The agent generated the plots using R code rather than copying existing images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the script contains the actual plotting and saving commands (e.g., 'png(...)', 'ggsave(...)') and does not use shell commands like 'cp' or 'wget' to place pre-existing images into the output directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The R script must be located at '/home/ga/RProjects/credit_risk_analysis.R'. The model must be a Random Forest (using packages like 'ranger' or 'randomForest'). The metrics file '/home/ga/RProjects/output/model_metrics.csv' must contain Accuracy and AUC (expected to be > 0.65). The variable importance plot must be saved to '/home/ga/RProjects/output/variable_importance.png' and the PDP for 'months_loan_duration' to '/home/ga/RProjects/output/pdp_duration.png'. The script should use 'set.seed(123)' for a 70/30 train-test split.
